pattern: Classifying a box is just two independent boolean checks -- size and mass -- combined into one of four labels, so the whole solution is straightforward branching arithmetic with no loops or data structures needed.
hints: Think of 'bulky' and 'heavy' as two separate yes/no questions you answer first, and only worry about the final label afterward.
A box counts as bulky if any single side alone reaches the size limit, or if the three sides multiplied together reach the volume limit -- check both ways before ruling it out.
Once you know the two booleans, the four-way answer is just a lookup: both true, only one true (pick which), or neither.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible box on every dimension, light
one dimension alone hits the size threshold, mass stays low
just under both the size and volume thresholds, and just under the mass threshold
mass exactly at the heavy threshold, dimensions small
no single side reaches the size limit, but the product of the three sides reaches the volume limit
both the size condition and the mass condition are satisfied at once
every value pinned at its maximum allowed bound
dimensions moderate and mass just under the heavy threshold, so neither condition fires

stress.description: random dimensions and mass spanning the full allowed range, including values that straddle both thresholds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    width = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    height = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    mass = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [length, width, height, mass]
